singular_emotional_truth: A big chorus that must not bury the vocal.
negative_constraints: Do not let the chorus become a wall of sound.
Do not let stock loops dominate the song identity.
overall_diagnosis: Overall mix readiness 71.8/100. Static balance 72.0/100; dynamic movement 23.4/100. The mix is more balanced than it is alive — invest in section contrast and rides, not more EQ. 1 mute/chop candidate(s) flagged; subtraction may help more than addition.
biggest_risks: Chorus 1 may not lift enough relative to the previous section (only +0.3 dB and -0.02 width). Target roughly +3 to +5 dB and +0.05 to +0.10 width via supporting elements and automation, not master level.
best_opportunities: Resolve 1 critical masking conflict(s) by depth placement and subtraction.
Create real section contrast with automation (bloom/intimacy) instead of master level.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: heard element in the intimate layer. Low-mid mud is elevated.

actions.setting: High-pass ~80 Hz. Cut ~250 Hz by 1-2 dB (mud). Avoid large 12 kHz+ air boosts (keeps the vocal human, not glossy).
actions.reason: Clean low-end build-up and keep believable presence without hype.

actions.setting: Vintage Opto, target 2-3 dB gain reduction, slow-ish attack.
actions.reason: Stabilise the vocal while preserving the performance (invisible compression).

automation.move: Ride phrase endings +0.5 to +1 dB where the lyric drops, before adding compression.
automation.reason: Ramone-style vocal belief: performance rides before brute-force compression.

per_track_actions.send_reverb: Short room or slapback only, very low send (~-24 dB). Keep it close.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: heard element in the foreground layer. Low-mid mud is elevated.
heard element in the foreground layer. Low-mid mud is elevated.
heard element in the foreground layer. Low-mid mud is elevated.
felt element in the background layer. Low-mid mud is elevated.

actions.setting: High-pass ~250 Hz; low-pass the top if it is purely atmospheric.
actions.reason: Filter felt elements so they support without crowding.

automation.move: Open the filter and lift the reverb send only in chorus sections; pull back in verses.
automation.reason: Use the element as movement (felt), not a constant heard layer.

per_track_actions.send_reverb: Hall or long plate, higher send (~-10 dB), filtered and diffuse.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: felt element in the background layer. Wider than a felt element should be.

actions.reason: Felt/atmospheric element should not occupy the same width as heard elements.

actions.setting: High-pass ~250 Hz; low-pass the top if it is purely atmospheric.
actions.reason: Filter felt elements so they support without crowding.

actions.setting: Consider chopping into transition gestures and muting the continuous bed; reserve for pre-chorus/bridge.
actions.reason: Re-contextualise the imported loop instead of running it as a finished record inside the record.

automation.move: Open the filter and lift the reverb send only in chorus sections; pull back in verses.
automation.reason: Use the element as movement (felt), not a constant heard layer.

per_track_actions.send_reverb: Hall or long plate, higher send (~-10 dB), filtered and diffuse.
warnings: Imported loop: do not let it dominate the song identity. Non-destructive duplicate before chopping.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: structural element in the foreground layer.

actions.setting: High-pass ~30 Hz; control 200-350 Hz if boxy.
actions.reason: Tighten the low end without thinning the body.

actions.setting: Vintage Opto, gentle 2-4 dB GR for consistent sustain.
Carve a small dip where the kick thumps (complementary to kick).
actions.reason: Resolve kick/bass low-end conflict by carving, not stacking.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: structural element in the foreground layer.

actions.setting: Shape sub (~50-70 Hz) vs beater (~3-5 kHz); leave room for bass.
actions.reason: Define the kick without masking the bass.

per_track_actions.diagnosis: structural element in the foreground layer.

actions.setting: Light tonal shaping only; preserve transient detail and room realism.
actions.reason: Halee naturalism: keep the kit believable.

per_section_actions.name: Chorus 1
per_section_actions.contrast_warning: Chorus 1 may not lift enough relative to the previous section (only +0.3 dB and -0.02 width). Target roughly +3 to +5 dB and +0.05 to +0.10 width via supporting elements and automation, not master level.

automation_plan.name: Chorus 1
automation_plan.contrast_warning: Chorus 1 may not lift enough relative to the previous section (only +0.3 dB and -0.02 width). Target roughly +3 to +5 dB and +0.05 to +0.10 width via supporting elements and automation, not master level.

moves.move: Phrase-level rides +0.5 to +1.5 dB before compression.
Open and lift only in choruses; pull back in verses.
Open and lift only in choruses; pull back in verses.

mute_candidates.reason: Width crowding in 'verse_1': reserve the widest placement for one element; push or mute the rest.

source_material_warnings.warning: Printed instrument bounce: note/patch/envelope changes require re-rendering from the original MIDI, not audio editing.
Imported loop/sample: verify it is not accepted at full width in the foreground by default. Re-contextualise into the song's depth and tone.
Stereo image is wide; consider narrowing and placing it in the midground/background rather than full-width foreground.
Low crest factor suggests the loop is pre-compressed/mastered; it may make live tracks feel small next to it.

production_vs_mix_boundaries.reason: 3 wide, forward elements share the stereo image in section 'verse_1'. The mix may feel crowded even if levels balance.
production_vs_mix_boundaries.best_fix: Mute or push decorative/wide elements to the background rather than EQ-ing all of them.

production_vs_mix_boundaries.issue: Chorus 1 does not lift but is already dense
production_vs_mix_boundaries.reason: Density increases without an increase in space/width; adding more will not create lift.
production_vs_mix_boundaries.best_fix: Create contrast by subtraction before the section, then bloom space at entry.

warnings: Chorus 1 may not lift enough relative to the previous section (only +0.3 dB and -0.02 width). Target roughly +3 to +5 dB and +0.05 to +0.10 width via supporting elements and automation, not master level.

next_pass.title: Vocal belief
next_pass.detail: Ride the lead vocal phrase-by-phrase before adding compression.

next_pass.title: Section contrast
next_pass.detail: Chorus 1 do not lift enough. Create lift through supporting elements and automation (bloom on entry, intimacy in verses), not master level.

next_pass.title: Stereo loop / width control
next_pass.detail: Splice Texture Loop appear full-width/foregrounded. Narrow to ~35-50%, filter, or push to the background so they are felt, not heard.

next_pass.title: Depth cleanup
next_pass.detail: Too many elements occupy the foreground. Move supporting guitars/keys/pads to the midground instead of EQ-ing everything.

next_pass.title: Low-end definition
next_pass.detail: Kick and bass share sub energy. Carve complementary space or sidechain the bass subtly to the kick.
